image_prompt: A dramatic 19th-century Romanticism marine oil painting on canvas, in the sublime tradition of J.M.W. Turner and Ivan Aivazovsky, filling the entire frame. The sailing ship pitches and rolls heavily with the swell, sails luffing in the wind, but keeps its solid form. Dramatic, powerful, cinematic storm — wind and water in motion, the vessel intact.

title: Rock-Hewn Church - Ethiopian Lalibela (AI Animated)
image_prompt: The medieval rock-hewn Church of Saint George (Bete Giyorgis) at Lalibela, Ethiopia, 12th-13th century, carved as a single monolithic Greek cross sunk down into the living red volcanic tuff rock, photographed in-situ at golden hour so the carved bedrock fills the entire frame edge to edge. An elevated three-quarter view looking down into the deep rectangular trench surrounding the cruciform church; the cross-shaped roof carved with concentric crosses in relief, tall narrow windows cut into the warm reddish-brown stone walls, weathered hand-chiselled texture. Warm low golden sunlight rakes across one side of the carved rock, casting deep soft shadows into the surrounding pit; a clear pale sky above, a few green shrubs at the rim. Earthy ochre, terracotta and gold palette, ancient sacred atmosphere. no museum, no display case, no glass, no pedestal, no gallery wall, no spotlight, no museum label, no plain studio background, the carved rock church fills the entire frame edge to edge.
video_prompt: A gentle seamless cycle that ends exactly where it began. Warm golden sunlight slowly shifts and breathes across the carved red rock, the soft shadows in the surrounding trench deepening and easing back. A faint haze of dust and a few high clouds drift slowly across the pale sky. The green shrubs at the rim sway softly in a light breeze, and a single bird glides across the distance and away. The monolithic rock church and the bedrock hold perfectly still and solid — only the light, the drifting haze and the swaying shrubs move, settling back to the opening frame. Serene, ancient, sacred.
